summary.what: Refuse timbers log on a dirty tree
summary.why: v0.22.7 still warned-and-proceeded when the tree was dirty, which let phantom entries reach upstream: an aborted gated commit leaves staged changes in the index, then timbers log auto-commits an entry that pathspec-scopes to the entry file only — the entry rides the old HEAD and the feature work stays unstaged. A field report from an agent in Constructured/osprey-strike observed two such phantoms in one session against v0.22.7. The existing warning text already told users to 'commit first to avoid phantom entries'; the change makes the tool enforce what the warning was asking for.
summary.how: Replaced the printer.Warn at cmd/timbers/log.go:103 with a UserError return guarded by !flags.dryRun (so --dry-run remains usable for inspecting an entry mid-debugging). The error message names the likely trigger (aborted gated commit), the diagnostic command (git diff --cached), and the --dry-run escape. Deliberately did NOT add --allow-dirty: the protocol has no case for logging uncommitted work, and the flag would reopen the exact footgun the refusal closes. Reworked TestLogDirtyTreeWarning → TestLogDirtyTreeRefuses with three subtests (refusal, dry-run-succeeds, clean-succeeds); the refusal subtest asserts no entry files are created on the failure path. Mutation-checked mentally: reverting to printer.Warn would make the refusal subtest fail since it expects cmd.Execute() != nil.

notes: Filed companion beads timbers-cwa (gate-refusal hint about staged-changes-in-index) and timbers-cs0 (auto-detect cross-agent debt in the gate). Considered Option B from the report (create entry but skip auto-commit) and rejected: the trigger is the gate ABORTING commits, so if the next commit also aborts the entry sits dirty indefinitely and dirty entries compound. Refusal is cleaner.
